self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can keep the canonical truth anchor and workbench detail useful by resolving a known structured historical Runtime follow-up directly instead of treating it as a broken deferred-contract case.
self_improvement.mechanism: This DEEP bounded result adds active-bounded legacy Runtime follow-up compatibility to the shared state resolver and workbench follow-up detail fallback.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this DEEP case, the structured Scientify legacy Runtime follow-up resolved as broken in the canonical report and as unreadable in the workbench because `n/a - active bounded follow-up` was treated like a missing deferred re-entry contract path.
self_improvement.expected_effect: Future report, composition, and workbench work can inspect that structured historical Runtime follow-up directly without weakening deferred follow-up gates.

decision.rationale: The shared truth anchor should resolve the structured historical Scientify Runtime follow-up as read-only historical state instead of a false broken deferred-contract case.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
